title: Form 17 — Notice of Case Conference, Settlement Conference, or Trial Management Conference
description: Used to schedule and give notice of a case conference, settlement conference, or trial management conference in an Ontario family law proceeding. Required under Family Law Rules Rule 17.

steps.title: Court & Case Information
steps.description: Enter the court file details. This form tells the court and the other party when and where the conference is scheduled.

fields.label: Courthouse
options: St. Catharines — Superior Court of Justice
Thunder Bay — Superior Court of Justice
fields.helpText: Select the courthouse where your family law case is being heard.

fields.label: Court file number
fields.helpText: Leave blank if you do not yet have one. It will be assigned when you file your application.

fields.label: Applicant's full legal name
Respondent's full legal name

steps.title: Type of Conference
steps.description: Select which type of conference you are scheduling. Each serves a different purpose in the family law process.

fields.label: What type of conference are you scheduling?
fields.helpText: Not sure which type? See the descriptions below.

fields.label: What does each type mean?
fields.content:
**Case Conference** — Usually the first meeting with a judge. The judge helps identify the issues, encourages settlement, and gives directions on next steps. Required before most motions can be brought.

**Settlement Conference** — A more focused meeting where a judge actively helps both parties try to resolve the case. Usually held after a case conference. Both parties must come prepared with their best settlement offer.

**Trial Management Conference** — Held just before trial to make sure both parties are ready. The judge reviews the issues going to trial, identifies witnesses, and makes procedural orders. Required before any trial.

fields.label: Is this your first conference in this case?

options.label: Yes — this is the first conference
No — there have been previous conferences

fields.label: Briefly describe what happened at the last conference
fields.placeholder: e.g. At the case conference on March 15, 2025, Justice Smith made a temporary order for child support of $800/month. The parties were directed to exchange financial disclosure and attend a settlement conference.

steps.title: Conference Date & Details
steps.description: Enter the date, time, and location for the conference. Contact the court clerk to schedule the date before completing this form.

fields.label: Date of the conference
fields.helpText: Contact the court clerk first to book a date. You must serve this notice on the other party at least 6 days before the conference.

fields.label: Time of the conference
fields.helpText: As confirmed by the court clerk.

fields.label: Location (courtroom or room number, if known)
fields.placeholder: e.g. Room 4B, 393 University Ave, Toronto ON M5G 1E6
fields.helpText: Leave blank if the court clerk has not yet assigned a room. The courthouse address is enough.

fields.label: Format of the conference

options.label: Hybrid — some in person, some remote

fields.helpText: Confirm the format with the court clerk. Many Ontario courts now offer video conferences.

fields.label: Video conference link or dial-in information
fields.placeholder: e.g. Zoom link: https://ontario.zoom.us/j/123456789 or call-in: 1-647-374-4685

steps.title: Issues for the Conference
steps.description: List the issues you want the conference to address. The judge will focus on what you identify here.

fields.label: What issues do you want to address at this conference? (select all that apply)
fields.helpText: Check everything that applies. You don't need to resolve all issues — just identify what is in dispute.

options.label: Who stays in the family home

fields.label: Describe the other issue
fields.placeholder: e.g. Division of a specific pension, passport for child travel

fields.label: Are there any issues that are already agreed between the parties?

options.label: No — all listed issues are still in dispute

fields.label: Describe what has already been agreed
fields.placeholder: e.g. The parties have agreed that the children will continue to attend the same school. Parenting time and child support amounts are still in dispute.

steps.title: Orders You Are Seeking
steps.description: Tell the court what you want the judge to order or direct at this conference.

fields.label: Are you asking the judge to make any temporary orders at this conference?
fields.helpText: At a case conference or settlement conference, a judge can make temporary orders on consent or on a motion. Identify if you need something in the short term.

options.label: No — just asking for directions and next steps

fields.label: Describe the temporary order(s) you are asking for
fields.placeholder: e.g. A temporary order that the children reside primarily with me pending trial, and that the Respondent pay interim child support of $750/month.

fields.label: What procedural directions are you asking the judge to give?
fields.helpText: Directions are instructions from the judge about how the case should proceed.

options.label: Set a timetable for exchanging documents and evidence
Order the other party to provide financial disclosure
Order an expert report (e.g. parenting assessment)

fields.label: Describe the other direction you are seeking
fields.placeholder: e.g. Order that both parties complete parenting education program before next conference

steps.title: Background Facts
steps.description: Provide a brief summary of the facts. The judge reads this before the conference to understand your situation.

fields.label: Brief history of the relationship and family
fields.placeholder: e.g. The parties were married on June 12, 2015 in Toronto and separated on February 1, 2024. There are two children: Alex (age 10) and Sam (age 7). The parties lived in the family home at 123 Main St, Toronto until separation. The Applicant remains in the family home with the children. The Respondent moved to an apartment nearby.
fields.helpText: Keep it factual and brief — 3 to 5 sentences. The judge uses this to understand the basics of your case before the conference.

fields.label: What are the current arrangements for the children and finances?
fields.placeholder: e.g. The children currently live primarily with the Applicant on a temporary basis. The Respondent has parenting time every other weekend. No support is currently being paid. There is no existing court order — these are informal arrangements only.
fields.helpText: Describe what is actually happening right now, even if there is no formal order.

fields.label: What is your main concern going into this conference?
fields.placeholder: e.g. My main concern is stabilizing the children's living situation and getting a temporary support order in place. The other party has been refusing to provide any financial disclosure, which is preventing us from resolving support amounts.
fields.helpText: Be honest and specific. This helps the judge understand what matters most to you.

steps.title: Service & Confirmation
steps.description: You must serve this notice on the other party before the conference.

fields.label: How will you serve this notice on the other party?
fields.helpText: You must serve at least 6 days before the conference.

fields.label: When do you plan to serve the other party?
fields.helpText: Must be at least 6 days before the conference date.

fields.label: Conference brief reminder
fields.content: **Important:** You must also prepare and serve a **conference brief** at least 7 days before the conference. The brief summarizes your position on each issue. Ask the court clerk which brief form is required for your type of conference (usually Form 17A for case conferences, Form 17C for settlement conferences, or Form 17E for trial management conferences).

fields.label: Have you prepared your conference brief?

options.label: In progress — I am preparing it now
Not yet — I will prepare it before the deadline

fields.label: Declaration
fields.checkboxLabel: I confirm the information in this Notice of Conference is accurate. I understand this form will be filed with the Ontario court and served on the other party.

pdfMapping.notes: Form 17 — used for case conference, settlement conference, and trial management conference notices. Filed with the court and served on the other party. See ontario.ca/page/family-law-forms for current version.
